0.original: Chapter 10 Get the Action Habit Here's something leaders in every field agree on. 2 00:00:08,960 --> 00:00:14,929 There is a shortage of top-flight, expertly qualified persons to fill key positions. 3 00:00:15,199 --> 00:00:19,569 There really is, as the saying goes, plenty of room at the top. 4 00:00:19,920 --> 00:00:28,530 As one executive explained, there are many almost qualified people, but there is one success ingredient often missing, 5 00:00:28,639 --> 00:00:32,929 that is, the ability to get things done, to get results. 6 00:00:33,760 --> 00:00:39,889 Every big job, whether it be operating a business, high-level selling, in science, 7 00:00:39,920 --> 00:00:44,609 the military, or the government, requires a man who thinks action. 8 00:00:44,960 --> 00:00:52,450 Principal executives looking for a key person demand answers to questions like, will he do the job? 9 00:00:52,640 --> 00:00:54,370 Will he follow through? 10 00:00:54,560 --> 00:00:56,370 Is he a self-starter? 11 00:00:56,560 --> 00:00:59,810 Can he get results or is he just a talker? 12 00:01:00,640 --> 00:01:06,130 All these questions have one aim, to find out if the fellow is a man of action. 13 00:01:06,800 --> 00:01:09,170 Excellent ideas are not enough. 14 00:01:09,360 --> 00:01:19,250 An only fair idea acted upon and developed is one hundred percent better than a terrific idea that dies because it isn't followed up. 15 00:01:20,000 --> 00:01:26,850 The great self-made merchant John Wanamaker often said, Nothing comes merely by thinking about it. 16 00:01:27,360 --> 00:01:28,289 Think of it. 17 00:01:28,480 --> 00:01:37,569 Everything we have in this world, from satellites to skyscrapers to baby food, is just an idea acted upon. 18 00:01:38,240 --> 00:01:45,890 As you study people, both the successful and the just average, you find they fall into two classes. 19 00:01:46,240 --> 00:01:48,289 The successful are active. 20 00:01:48,400 --> 00:01:50,929 We'll call them activationists. 21 00:01:51,120 --> 00:01:55,970 The just average, the mediocre, the unsuccessful, are passive. 22 00:01:56,160 --> 00:01:58,690 We'll call them passivationists. 23 00:01:59,760 --> 00:02:04,050 We can discover a success principle by studying both groups. 24 00:02:04,240 --> 00:02:06,770 Mr. Activationist is a doer. 25 00:02:06,879 --> 00:02:12,130 He takes action, gets things done, follows through on ideas and plans. 26 00:02:12,479 --> 00:02:15,490 Mr. Passivationist is a donter. 27 00:02:15,599 --> 00:02:21,010 He postpones doing things until he has proved he shouldn't or can't do them, 28 00:02:21,039 --> 00:02:22,850 or until it's too late. 29 00:02:23,679 --> 00:02:29,100 The difference between Mr. Activationist and Mr. Passivationist shows through in countless little ways.
0.b1: Chapter 10: Develop the Habit of Taking Action. Leaders everywhere agree on this point.
0.cn: 第10章 养成行动的习惯。这是各行各业的领导者都认同的一点。
0.ai_notes:
'Get the Action Habit': to make taking action a regular part of your daily routine.
'leaders in every field': people who are successful and influential in their specific industry or area of work.
'shortage': a situation where there is not enough of something that is needed.
'top-flight': of the highest quality or rank; excellent.
'plenty of room at the top': a common expression meaning there are always opportunities for talented people to reach the highest levels of success.
'success ingredient': a key factor or quality that is necessary to achieve success.
'get things done': to complete tasks or achieve goals effectively.
'thinks action': a way of describing someone who focuses on doing things rather than just planning or worrying.
'Principal executives': the most important or high-ranking leaders in an organization.
'follow through': to continue an action or task until it is completely finished.
'self-starter': a person who is motivated to start tasks and work without needing someone else to tell them what to do.
'talker': someone who speaks a lot about their plans but does not actually do the work.
'man of action': a person who prefers to do things rather than just talk or think about them.
'only fair': average or acceptable, but not excellent.
'terrific': extremely good or impressive.
'dies': in this context, it means an idea is forgotten or never used.
'followed up': to take further action to ensure something is completed or successful.
'John Wanamaker': a famous 19th-century American merchant and pioneer of the modern department store.
'self-made': someone who achieved success through their own hard work rather than inheriting wealth or status.
'satellites to skyscrapers': a way of saying 'everything from the most complex technology to the largest buildings'.
'average': ordinary or typical; not particularly good or bad.
'activationists': a term created by the author to describe people who are active and take initiative.
'mediocre': not very good; of average or low quality.
'passivationists': a term created by the author to describe people who are passive and avoid taking action.
'doer': a person who acts rather than just thinking or talking.
'donter': a playful term created by the author for someone who avoids doing things.
'postpones': to delay or put off doing something until a later time.
'countless': too many to be counted; very many.
0.remarks:
'Get the Action Habit': 将采取行动变成日常习惯。
'leaders in every field': 在各自领域成功且有影响力的人。
'shortage': 某种必需品供应不足的情况。
'top-flight': 最高质量或等级的；优秀的。
'plenty of room at the top': 一句常用语，意为有才华的人总有机会达到最高成就。
'success ingredient': 取得成功所必需的关键因素或品质。
'get things done': 有效地完成任务或目标。
'thinks action': 形容一个人专注于做事而非仅仅计划或担忧。
'Principal executives': 组织中最重要的或最高级别的领导者。
'follow through': 持续执行任务直到完全结束。
'self-starter': 能够主动开始工作而无需他人督促的人。
'talker': 只会谈论计划却不付诸行动的人。
'man of action': 倾向于做事而非仅仅空谈或思考的人。
'only fair': 一般的或可以接受的，但不优秀。
'terrific': 非常好或令人印象深刻的。
'dies': 在此语境下，指想法被遗忘或从未被使用。
'followed up': 采取进一步行动以确保事情完成或成功。
'John Wanamaker': 19世纪著名的美国商人，现代百货公司的先驱。
'self-made': 靠自己的努力而非继承财富或地位获得成功的人。
'satellites to skyscrapers': 形容“从最复杂的技术到最大的建筑”的一切事物。
'average': 普通的或典型的；不是特别好或坏。
'activationists': 作者创造的术语，指积极主动的人。
'mediocre': 不太好的；质量平平或低劣的。
'passivationists': 作者创造的术语，指被动且避免采取行动的人。
'doer': 倾向于行动而非仅仅思考或谈论的人。
'donter': 作者创造的俏皮词汇，指逃避做事的人。
'postpones': 推迟或延后做某事。
'countless': 无数的；多得数不清的。
